title: Detection of FLAG ULK1 and Endogenous ATG13 Interaction
author: Yongjia Duan

steps.title: Collect cell lysates using NP-40 lysis...
steps.instruction: Collect cell lysates using NP-40 lysis buffer supplemented with protease and phosphatase inhibitors.

steps.title: Wash cells with cold PBS (1 mL per well)
steps.instruction: Wash cells with cold PBS (1 mL per well).

steps.title: Add 200 μL of NP-40 lysis...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μL of NP-40 lysis buffer supplemented with 1 mL of protease inhibitor cocktail and 1 mL of phosphatase inhibitor cocktail to each well.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 30 minutes, vortexing every 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 30 minutes, vortexing every 10 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 10...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 rpm for 10 minutes at 4°C to collect supernatant.

steps.title: Perform FLAG pulldown using anti-FLAG M2 affinity beads
steps.instruction: Perform FLAG pulldown using anti-FLAG M2 affinity beads.

steps.title: Transfer 0
steps.instruction: Transfer 0.6 mg of cell lysate to a clean tube.

steps.title: Add 30 μL of anti-FLAG M2 affinity beads
steps.instruction: Add 30 μL of anti-FLAG M2 affinity beads.

steps.title: Incubate for 4 hours at 4°C...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 4 hours at 4°C with end-over-end rotation (25 rpm).
1. Centrifuge the tube at 1,000 g for 1 minute to pellet beads. 2. Remove supernatant and wash beads three times with 1 mL of NP-40 lysis buffer.
1. Resuspend beads in 40 μL of 2X LDS buffer, 1 mM DTT, and 1 μL of beta-mercaptoethanol. 2. Heat at 95°C for 5 minutes to elute proteins.
1. Pass the eluate through a Spin-X® column to separate beads from the eluted proteins.
